title: The 2024 Box Office Is Burying Its Dead With the Horror Genre - What Happened
text: Horror films dominated the box office in 2022-2023 with blockbusters like M3GAN, Smile, and Evil Dead Rise generating substantial profits. However, 2024 has reversed this trajectory dramatically, with most horror releases underperforming significantly. Several highly-anticipated films failed to meet expectations: The First Omen didn't even double its $30 million budget despite positive reviews. Abigail struggled to recover its production costs. Immaculate earned only $22 million against its $9 million budget. Imaginary, Night Swim, and Lisa Frankenstein all disappointed at the box office. Root causes include repetitive subgenres where religious horror and supernatural movies involving demons, possession and similar Judeo-Christian concepts have saturated the market, with too many similar films releasing close together. Viewers appear bored with horror and increasingly willing to wait for streaming releases rather than visit theaters. The industry needs wild outside the box ideas and truly fresh concepts. Successful films like M3GAN succeeded through originality and unique hooks, while derivative projects failed to attract audiences.
